findings.title: Unescaped DB string ({item.name}) rendered as JSX child in an innerHTML-applied fragment
findings.description: Identical pattern to step 04. ProductList.render (L28) interpolates the free-form DB string `{item.name}` as a JSX child. The @jiso/server JSX runtime does not escape text children (packages/server/src/jsx-runtime.ts renderJsxChildren L79-84 returns String(children); only attributes are escaped). ProductList is fragmentTarget:true and its output is wired into `<fw-fragment target="product-list">` (app.ts renderShopPage / submitAddToCart fragmentRenderers), which the client applies via innerHTML / insertAdjacentHTML with no sanitization (packages/runtime/src/response-fragment-apply.ts L58, L62). Markup in a product name therefore becomes stored XSS. Not directly exploitable in the tutorial because names are static fixtures and addToCart never writes a name from input — MEDIUM/medium confidence; rises to HIGH if names become admin/vendor/user-controlled. The reference examples/commerce app avoids this by rendering only id/stock as children and escaping free-form data. The scanner's insecure-crypto flag on L11/L42 is a false positive (comment lines; the only crypto in the path is correct HMAC-SHA256 CSRF).
findings.recommendation: Escape DB/user strings rendered as children, e.g. `{escapeHtml(item.name)}`, or fix the JSX runtime to auto-escape string text children while keeping a raw type for pre-rendered HTML (component renders, csrfField).

refusal.reason: Analyzed all five target files fully, plus traced the relevant framework dataflow (jsx-runtime, html, csrf, response-fragment-apply) and compared against the reference commerce app. The two generated/ files were returned with empty findings per the explicit out-of-scope rule for generated artifacts, not due to discomfort.
